confidence_tiers.CONFIRMED: directly supported by a cited primary or secondary public source
confidence_tiers.PROBABLE: strong inference from 2+ corroborating sources; no single record states the whole claim
confidence_tiers.POSSIBLE: plausible from one source or logical inference only
confidence_tiers.UNVERIFIED: noted, not corroborated by any source

critical_correction.summary: Dale William Spence Sr. is NOT himself an English immigrant. He is American-born in Beaumont, Texas (~1934-1936). The 'came from England' family tradition attaches to his FATHER — Hunter's paternal great-grandfather — an English-born man (name unknown) who immigrated to Beaumont circa 1920-1935.
critical_correction.confidence: CONFIRMED for Dale Sr. being American-born; PROBABLE for father-as-immigrant interpretation

identity.generation: Hunter's grandfather (Hunter → Dale Jr. → Dale Sr.)

birth.year_basis: Beaumont High School state track champion 1952; standard age at state championship is 17-18; implies birth 1934-1936

mother.given_name_source: family memory; supported by 48-dovie-spence-deep.md research pass
mother.nationality_source: Behind the Name classifies 'Dovie' as American South female given name — no English or Scottish parallel; https://www.behindthename.com/name/dovie

father.birth_region_england: NE England — Yorkshire, County Durham, or Northumberland (highest Spence surname concentration in England)
father.immigration_driver: Oil/industrial labor, Beaumont-Port Arthur corridor (Spindletop 1901 triggered British worker migration)
